(II) Ohana Yogas or Combinations of wealth.
(iii) Penury Yogas i.e. the combinations whith give poverty.
Before describing these Yogas. let me deS<:ribe the meaning of a
Yoga or a combination. The combinations are of the following four
types:
(1) Exchange of House: Two planets if OCOJPY each othefs
sign. are said to have exchanged houses or signs. This is
the most powerful combination.
(2) Mutual Aspect: Mutual aspect between two planets is
the second type of combination. This combination is the
second most powerful combination.
(3) If a planet is aspected by its dis:positor it is the third type of
combination. It is less powerful than the above two types
of combination.
(4) Conjunction: Two planets in the same sign are said to be
in Conjunction. Fot this type of combiMtion to be effective
the two planets should be in the same sign and
simultaneously in the same house when seen in the Chalit
Chakta. This is the least powerful amongst the four types
of combinations.
